basics.name: Tim Abell 🤓
basics.label: Available Now ~ 💻 C# + Rust  💻 Lead + Dev  💻  Contracts + Projects 💻
basics.summary:
🤓 Software consultant, lead engineer, nerd & coder.
🤓 Founder of charmconsulting.co.uk and rustworkshop.co
🤓 Consultants in C#, Azure and related tech
🤓 Building RustLang agency.
🤓 blogs at timwise.co.uk
🤓 Writes tests first with vim.

Recruiters: this CV is not authorised for distribution to your clients. Please contact me for permission to represent me and for a separate copy containing an authorisation code & GPG signature. Thanks.  Prospective clients: if you receive my CV from a third-party without the authorisation code & GPG signature then I haven't given them my permission to represent me. Learn more: https://charmconsulting.co.uk/recruiters

work.name: Charm Consulting Ltd
work.summary: We've been contributing to the success of software projects since 2012. We love projects that deliver value to you and your end users. If you're thinking of starting a new software project, legacy or greenfield then be sure to reduce your risk of failure by booking a free expert advice call with us first with no obligation to purchase further services. We want you to succeed.

work.name: Rust Workshop
work.summary: Building a new specialist software development agency and community and the popular and impressive RustLang programming language. || https://rustworkshop.co/ ||

work.name: NewDay
work.summary: Lead dev for 3 engineers on a modern Azure microservices event sourced message based system. Collaborating with several other peer teams, product owners etc to support and enable the team to deliver quality software at a sustainable pace.

work.name: NewDay
work.summary: Providing software engineering services to assist building a high-performance event-sourced message based system in dotnet. Azure cloud etc.

work.name: 101 Ways
work.summary: Provided development services on behalf of 101 Ways to DrDoctor. Several small projects to improve DrDoctor systems; operating within existing client delivery teams. Provided 101 Ways with a detailed set of insights as a basis for further assisting their client with improvements to their development organisation.

work.name: DrDoctor
work.summary:
Three small projects on behalf of 101 Ways.
- Security patching
- Improvements to a PowerBI report-embedding system (dotnet)
- Research and recommendations for improvements to internal GDPR request handling processes

work.name: eSynergy Solutions
work.summary: Contributing to eSynergy's success as a valued lead associate to deliver projects for their clients. Contributions: delivery on projects, technical screening, speaking at internal and lead-digital events.

work.name: DogLost
work.summary:
DogLost.co.uk is the biggest lost & found database + website for the UK, plus a network of volunteers giving support and advice to people going through the stress of losing their beloved dog.
I've become part of the trusted leadership team and am helping the organisation make the best of technology and tools on restricted resources.

work.name: DfE Digital and Technology
work.summary:
Part of a team building a tracking and payment system for the reformed continuing teacher professional development system. Contributions included:
- encouraging a focus on immediate delivery and agile practices with an inexperienced product manager
- contributions to the ruby on rails codebase
- weekly reporting back to eSynergy (my direct client) on the progress and goings-on in the project to help them keep the finger on the pulse of their project (reportedly very useful)
- reviewing code, contributing to discussions on how to build the system

work.name: National Careers Service
work.summary:
I joined this rescue project to assist the transition from first successful delivery to ongoing excellence in delivery. As well as being involved in front-line coding to improve the project, I've assisted the team in making the process, relationship and architectural transformations needed to become a top notch delivery team in a challenging enterprise environment.

We've been iterating on a c# aspnet dotnet-core application deployed to Azure with multiple microservices and Azure Function Apps. We've been working to improve the data, quality modelling and storage in the Azure CosmosDB and hosted SQL Server.

To support this effort I have extensively researched industry best practices for dealing with low quality projects; you can read the full write up here https://timwise.co.uk/2020/07/09/approaches-to-refactoring-and-technical-debt/

work.name: DfE Digital and Technology
work.summary:
Part of a GDS-style team at the UK Department for Education building a service to help potential teachers find postgraduate teacher training. https://www.gov.uk/find-postgraduate-teacher-training-courses
Coding: .net core, c#, bash, postgres, ruby on rails, js, microservices, terraform, azure.... it's all up on https://github.com/DFE-Digital/ - been learning a lot of new tech as I build!
Team: a high performing multi-discipline team, from user research through support to design and dev, all focussed on user-needs. Lots of pairing & mentoring. I wrote a piece on delivery managers which you might find interesting: https://timwise.co.uk/2019/07/08/why-every-team-needs-a-delivery-manager/

"I formally wanted a show a huge amount of gratitude for Tim Abell. Tim has been on #twd_find for 2 years and has had a massive positive impact on the service, team and ultimately, our users. You will be missed mate! " ~ Dan Mitchell, Delivery Manager

work.name: Capita IT Professional Services
work.summary: Referral. Part of a team working on an ASP.NET MVC, SQL Server project for a government department. Worked to ensure my direct client and their customer’s representatives were satisfied with the improvements made to the system. Suggested improvements based on my experience, but allowed the client to decide what was appropriate to take forward. Unfortunately I'm unable to discuss this role further due to NDA. Gained SC level clearance.

work.name: Solidsoft Reply
work.summary: Repeat business. Worked on adding functionality to a low-latency high-throughput Azure Service Fabric application written in C# with an extensive regression-test suite.

work.name: MDK INNOVATION LIMITED
work.summary:
Repeat business extending the new case-management system for the Ministry of Justice created in my last contract with MDK.

Worked on their ASP.NET MVC / C# / Bootstrap / SQL Server 2012 / EF6 code. Wrote code to migrate and merge live data from additional legacy systems into the new production database created at the end of my previous contract with MDK. Worked on database-schema migrations with ReadyRoll to support new application code.

work.name: Solidsoft Reply
work.summary:
Referral. Joined a team building a SQL Server backed SMTP security relay and associated ASP.NET management console for a Solidsoft client.

This was a SCRUM / Agile team using test-driven development (TDD) to deliver usable increments of working software on a regular basis. The project had a full end-to-end regression-test suite using Selenium to drive the relay’s admin console and Specflow to define the tests (a BDD/Gherkin tool). This project helped shape my thinking on nregression testing, learn more at https://blog.timwise.co.uk/2018/03/13/regression-tests-for-asp-net-sql-projects/

work.name: MDK INNOVATION LIMITED
work.summary: Part of a remote-only team building greenfield case-management system in ASP.NET MVC 5 / Bootstrap / SQL Server 2012 for use by the Ministry of Justice. I contributed to the full stack, with an emphasis on the database & data migration from the legacy system. I intoduced ReadyRoll to handle subsequent database upgrades and a new database-build process. I assisted the team with the switch from TFS to git. I improved documentation to assist future developers / maintainers / support staff.

work.name: 3chillies
work.summary: I worked on an ASP.NET MVC data-collection site for a client, including workflow and versioning, backed by Entity Framework 6.1 & SQL Server 2012. This is a complete replacement for a legacy system on different technologies. Layered architecture, making use of the Command-Query pattern (aka CQRS) at the data access layer.

work.name: Syzygy
work.summary: Worked on an ASP.NET based CMS site. Integration with SOAP WCF web service, making use of TDD and Moq. Refactoring of existing library’s code to improve architecture and testability. Provided guidance on source-management strategy and shared my experience with organising projects as they migrate from SVN to git.

work.name: ProvenWorks
work.summary: Fixes and improvements to an ASP.NET C# Salesforce application hosted in Azure.

work.name: Oliver Marketing
work.summary: Worked on an ASP.NET MVC4 / SQL Server / EF4 / Ninject / AngularJS project as part of a team. The project is an intranet system for dynamically managing forms. A significant part of the work was parsing of the legacy form definitions (XML), much of which was done via test-driven development (TDD), and included creation of a suite of regression tests.

work.name: BGL Group
work.summary: Worked on an ASP.NET C# website as part of an agile team that made heavy use of pair-programming. Creation of custom pages on top of a CMS system.

work.name: Government Digital Service
work.summary: Before this contract I took a break from C# to learn the basics of Ruby on Rails; then at GDS I worked on the RoR applications behind gov.uk. I worked in two different agile/scrum teams and used TDD. I learned a huge amount from some very experienced Rails engineers and I gained exposure to MongoDB, nginx, puppet & vagrant. After this contract I returned to C# contracting reinvigorated and with a wider perspective.

work.name: Audatex UK
work.summary:
Project #1
-------------
Lead developer for a team of four contract and permanent developers. We created a greenfield C# / ASP.NET MVC (Razor) web-based product for the motor-insurance industry. I utilized Agile SCRUM methods to organise the team's planning and workload and in doing so provided a showcase of best practice to the broader organisation.

We built the product to run on load-balanced web servers, backed by Microsoft SQL Server 2012 accessed through Entity Framework (EF4) model-first. The product integrates with Audatex's existing Oracle 11g data store. On the frontend we utilized modern HTML, jQuery, AJAX, and CSS to provide a quality user experience, ably assisted by a design agency. We used Test Driven Development (TDD) and added integration tests through my knowledge of automated database builds.

Both projects used TFS 2010 for source control, continuous integration, and (with customisation) SCRUM process management including elements of Kanban.

Project #2 (contract extended)
-------------
Lead developer for a team of five, integrating a mobile solution with Audatex's existing systems. We provided an ASP.NET 4.5 WebAPI back-end, and delivered an iOS app written in Titanium / Javascript / HTML5 / CSS to be run on iPads and Android tablets. This was combined with some rework of the existing C# platform architecture (using NHibernate).

As the team lead and SCRUM master, I guided the team in utilizing SCRUM practices, and contributed as part of the development team to the planning and implementation of the codebase.

work.name: Signals Ltd
work.summary: Extended an existing C# / ASP.NET MVC 3 / SQL Server 2008 application for a significant Signals customer, and was involved in communication with the end customer. I brought Agile / SCRUM experience to the team, and added automated database builds.

work.name: PharmiWeb
work.summary: First contract role. I extended a proprietary CMS to provide for uploading and hosting of self-led presentations. I solved an interesting problem with HTML rendering variations which became the highest-voted answer to the question on Stack Overflow: http://stackoverflow.com/a/10137912/10245

work.name: PRISYMID
work.summary: Worked on customer-specific variations to the core product written in C# ASP.NET MVC / SQL Server 2008 / Oracle 10g. Some TDD. I became known as the "go to guy" by the incoming director of development for my visible attitude of getting things done and working hard to make improvements beyond the task at hand.

work.name: Caternet Ltd
work.summary: Worked on a SaaS product written in C# / ASP.NET MVC2 / SQL Server 2008. I gained experience of working in a highly effective Agile / SCRUM team.

work.name: emapsite.com
work.summary: I enjoyed 6 years with emapsite. I built a close working relationship with staff and clients, gaining the trust and respect of all and forging strong friendships. I was  responsible for website and intranet development (C#, ASP.NET, SQL Server 2005 and some Java; with clientside Javascript, CSS, jQuery). As this is a small company, my role was varied and covered all aspects of development and system administration. I introduced Continuous Integration with CruiseControl.NET and automated the database build and deployment. I brought in source control (svn), then migrated to git and trained the team on it.

work.name: Rhetorik
work.summary: Worked on all the in-house software. SQL Server 7 / 2000, MS Access 97 / 2000 / VBA, VB6, ASP Classic, C#.NET, ASP.NET.

work.name: Volume Direct Marketing
work.summary: Contact data management for physical/email marketing. Created utility programs in ASP Classic / Access 2000 / VBScript / VB6, including an SMTP mailer and DNS validator.

work.name: ICMA Centre
work.summary: Formerly ISMA Centre. Responsible for ISMA Centre's website created with ASP Classic & SQL Server '97 / 2000. I created a home-grown CMS in VB6 for the site. I introduced source control (in the same year “The Joel Test” was written!) I created extranets for prospective students, current students and Alumni. I also worked on internal VB6 / SQL Server 7 systems.

certificates.name: Microsoft Certified Technology Specialist in C# and ASP.NET

references.name: Nicholas Masters
references.reference: Working with Tim is a refreshing and new approach to working with development specialists. Not only is Tim an expert in his discipline, he also possesses extremely high levels of work ethic and commitment to continual development. Tim excels when working in a consultancy setting with his communication skills and ability to read the room. I would work with Tim again in a heartbeat .

references.name: George Kelly
references.reference: I've had the pleasure of working closely with Tim for the past year or so. Not only is Tim a first-class developer, but also someone who dedicates time to strengthen the wider team relationships. His passion towards his work is reflected in his focus on delivering quality outcomes. Tim has a friendly approach and has a talent at explaining technical terms to non-technical people. He constantly strives to expand his knowledge and develop technically. Tim has become a trusted associate and I look forward to continuing our relationship.

references.name: Fausta Cerezo-Gomez
references.reference: Tim brings a wide range of skills to the multidisciplinary team where we worked together.  He is not only technically strong on various fields including C#, and Ruby on Rails but he has a sensible and meticulous approach to problem-solving in other areas of the service delivery to users. He is an excellent communicator, and he is well-able to explain technical terms to non-technical people. I have observed how the Product Delivery Manager relies on his expertise for key stakeholders meetings. He is very pleasant and great to have in the team.

references.name: Rav Panesar
references.reference: Worked with Tim at Capita, He was an excellent developer that paid meticulous detail to requirements. Tim worked extremly well in the teamm always came up with great ideas and was always striving to learn new technologies throughout his time on the poject.

references.name: David Houliston
references.reference:
I enjoyed working with Tim, especially as he has an innovative approach, a thorough grasp of the development cycle and an encyclopaedic knowledge of computer languages. He is an excellent C# developer and produces good solutions in a short time. He also has a very enquiring mind and will suggest ways in which development can be done better.

He is very friendly and makes a point of getting to know those he works with at all levels.

references.name: Gary Keogh
references.reference:
Tim supplied subcontractor services to MDK Innovation as part of a small team working on the development of a new case management database system for a government department.

Tim made contributions across the whole technology stack, but his most notable role was taking the lead on managing the schemas and Entity Framework models involved in the project. This was a highly complex undertaking, because it involved both management of schema changes from design into development, and from development into production, while simultaneously developing data migration procedures for multiple predecessor systems. Tim addressed these problems with ingenuity and diligence, and the success of the project is due in no small part to his efforts.

He was also instrumental in increasing the efficiency of the team, by assisting with the transition to working with new collaboration software and helping us build a better understanding of agile methodologies.

Tim’s engagement ceased shortly after the second wave of system release and data migration was was taken into production, which represented the completion of the first phase of the project. Both operations were accomplished with minimal issues. I cannot recommend Tim highly enough; I would engage him again myself without hesitation if the opportunity arose.

references.name: Jeff Antram
references.reference: Tim was the original team lead and scrum master for the mobile app team I joined at Audatex. I learnt a lot from working with Tim and the project is still reaping the benefits of the processes and approach he put in place.

references.name: J Gerrard
references.reference: I worked with Tim and found him highly personable, professional, and proactive. With both an eye for quality solutions and an understanding of larger business objectives, he is an asset to any team. I hope to work with him again.

references.name: Sudhir Tibrewal
references.reference: Tim is a good Team Lead and listens to each individuals. He is good in managing the team and proactive. It was short time but good to work with him.

references.name: Jawad Umar
references.reference: Attention to detail and the ability to excel in both technical and non-technical aspects of development, Tim was a valued part of our team at Caternet. Both reliable and trustworthy Tim is able to translate business requirements into working software and always there to help team members.

projects.name: C# TDD example
projects.summary: Just a little exercise in writing C# with TDD.

projects.name: Entity Framework 6.1 Enum Lookup-Table Generator
projects.summary:
An open-source library published on github and made available through nuget.

Creates lookup tables and foreign key constraints based on the enums used in your model.

This makes up for a feature that's missing in Entity Framework 6.1

projects.name: Gitopolis (git multi-repo cli written in Rust)
